name: a-pattern-language
description: Christopher Alexander's 'A Pattern Language: Towns, Buildings, Construction' — a toolkit for designing spaces that feel alive, using 253 interconnected patterns for towns, buildings, rooms, and construction details.
useCase: Design or diagnose any space — from a room and house to a neighborhood and town — using 253 proven patterns for making places feel alive and functional.
knownRisks: The 253 patterns can overwhelm beginners — start with the 5-10 patterns most relevant to your specific project
Patterns are empirically grounded in 1970s observation — some urban patterns may need adaptation for contemporary contexts
The pattern language is a reference work, not a narrative — best used by reading the sections relevant to your project, not cover-to-cover
